quiz_description: Discover how you learn best - Visual, Auditory, Kinesthetic, or Reading/Writing

visual.name: Visual
visual.title: VISUAL LEARNER
visual.description:
You learn best through seeing and visualizing. Charts, diagrams, videos, and spatial arrangements help you understand and remember.

Growth comes from creating visual aids, mind maps, and using color to organize information.

auditory.name: Auditory
auditory.title: AUDITORY LEARNER
auditory.description:
You learn best through listening and speaking. Discussions, lectures, podcasts, and verbal explanations resonate with you.

Growth comes from reading aloud, joining study groups, and recording voice notes.

kinesthetic.name: Kinesthetic
kinesthetic.title: KINESTHETIC LEARNER
kinesthetic.description:
You learn best through doing and experiencing. Hands-on activities, movement, and physical engagement help you understand.

Growth comes from practice exercises, building models, and learning by doing.

reading_writing.name: Reading/Writing
reading_writing.title: READING/WRITING LEARNER
reading_writing.description:
You learn best through written words. Reading, note-taking, and writing essays or summaries help you process information.

Growth comes from detailed notes, reading widely, and writing to clarify your thinking.

questions.question: When learning something new, what helps you most?

options.text: Watching a video or looking at diagrams
Listening to an explanation or podcast

questions.question: How do you prefer to study for a test?
What helps you remember information best?
In a workshop, which activity would you prefer?
What describes your learning process best?

options.text: I need to see it to understand it
I need to hear it to understand it
I need to do it to understand it
I need to read about it to understand it
